# Patti Smith Real Estate, Pilot Hill, California

> El Dorado County real estate broker serving Pilot Hill and the Highway 49 ranch corridor. Specialist in ranch and acreage property, equestrian land, view estates near Folsom Lake, wells and septic, fire hazard zones, and Gold Country real estate.

## About
- Broker: Patti Smith (Patti Smith Real Estate, Independent; family-owned, own managing broker)
- License: CA DRE #01110483
- Office: 6180 State Highway 193, Georgetown, CA 95634
- Phone: (530) 368-0852
- Serving the Sierra Nevada foothills since 1992
- Highest completed sale: a $1.7M Pilot Hill estate with panoramic Folsom Lake views

## Pilot Hill, CA at a glance
- Unincorporated rural community in El Dorado County along Highway 49 between Coloma and Cool; population about 1,300; elevation about 1,175 feet, the lowest of the foothill communities; ZIP 95664
- Rolling oak-and-grassland ranch country rather than pine forest; warm summers, easy year-round access; high fire hazard severity zones
- Settled in the early 1850s as the mining camp Centerville; renamed Pilot Hill in 1854 for the landmark hill that guided travelers
- Home of the historic Bayley House (built 1861-1862 by A.J. Bayley for a transcontinental railroad that routed through Auburn instead; on the National Register) and Pilot Hill Grange No. 1, the first Grange in California (1870)
- Overlooks the South Fork arm of Folsom Lake near Salmon Falls; panoramic lake views drive premium estate values
- Water: private wells and septic on virtually all parcels
- Schools: a school-district boundary area, with Black Oak Mine Unified toward the Divide (Northside STEAM in Cool, Golden Sierra in Garden Valley) and Rescue Union toward El Dorado Hills; confirm assignment by parcel
- Typical market: ranchettes from the low-to-mid $400,000s; core homes on acreage roughly $500,000 to $800,000; estate and view properties from about $800,000 past $1,000,000
